title: UFO Day: Looking Up for Flying Fun
story: Every July 2, sky-watchers around the world celebrate World UFO Day by scanning the heavens for mysterious flying objects! People gather in groups to share stories about strange lights they've spotted, watch classic alien movies, and even dress up in shiny space costumes. Some families build cardboard flying saucers, while others organize stargazing parties with telescopes and snacks. Social media fills with photos of weird cloud formations and jokes about "little green visitors." Museums host special exhibits about unexplained sightings, and radio stations play spooky space-themed music all day long. Whether you believe in UFOs or just love the mystery, this holiday encourages everyone to look up and wonder what might be out there among the stars!
activity_question: What do people often use to look for UFOs on World UFO Day?
follow_up_question: What would you do if you spotted something strange flying in the sky?

title: Oops! It's I Forgot Day
story: July 2 is I Forgot Day, the perfect excuse for all your forgetful moments! On this silly holiday, people celebrate being human by admitting what they forgot recently. Some folks post funny confessions on social media about forgetting their keys, homework, or even their own birthday. Others use it as a guilt-free day to remember all those things they've been putting off. The best part? If someone asks why you didn't do something, you can just say "It's I Forgot Day!" and everyone understands.
activity_question: What do people often post on social media during I Forgot Day?
activity_choices: Funny confessions about things they forgot
activity_answer: Funny confessions about things they forgot
follow_up_question: What's the most important thing you've ever forgotten?
